title: quercus petraea subsp. iberica
intro: Quercus petraea subsp. iberica, also known as the Iberian sessile oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark of the Quercus petraea subsp. iberica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with properties that include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Quercus petraea subsp. iberica is often prepared as a decoction or infusions, and it can be found in herbal supplements or used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
